page: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores driven by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, it includes a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across compute, memory, and specialized acceleration tasks. The instance achieves strong results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, alongside high scores in Geekbench multi-core and software compilation. Memory performance is mixed, showing strong bandwidth for small cached blocks but average speeds for larger uncached blocks. The local NVMe storage and compute architecture support strong database operations, as evidenced by 62.3 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

The hardware configuration delivers top-tier performance for small to medium large language model inference. For a 135M parameter model, prompt processing reaches 12221.16 tokens per second, while a 7B model achieves 2270.07 tokens per second for prompt processing and 53.96 tokens per second for text generation. However, scaling to a large 70B model reveals a performance tradeoff, with text generation dropping to a weak 2.71 tokens per second. This profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for moderate-scale AI inference, graphics-intensive applications, and database-heavy workloads, while being less optimal for ultra-large model generation.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 64 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a local 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and networking baseline is 25 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier prompt processing speeds for small to medium LLMs, though text generation speeds drop significantly on 70B models. This instance is well-suited for medium-scale AI inference, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for AI inference and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 1880 GB NVMe storage for AI inference and database workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, local NVMe storage, and NVIDIA Ada Lovelace graphics for efficient machine learning inference.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.4 GHz
NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM for acceleration
1880 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Suited for AI inference, graphics rendering, and database workloads
